allies.rationale_text: Probabilistic-thinking helps margin-of-safety size the buffer from realistic uncertainty instead of the cleanest forecast path.
allies.source_quote: Probabilistic Thinking (Bayesian/Reference Class) quantifies the uncertainty that necessitates the margin.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias undermines margin-of-safety by selecting a safety estimate that confirms the current exposure is acceptable.
antagonists.source_quote: This prevents the acknowledgment of potential failure modes, disconfirming evidence, or systemic weaknesses that necessitate a larger MOS.

antagonists.rationale_text: Optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy directly undermines margin-of-safety by producing bold forecasts and thin buffers — the planning fallacy causes people to overestimate conjunctive success probability, making the required safety margin feel larger than necessary.
antagonists.source_quote: Optimism/Resilience: high optimism often leads to under-awareness of risks. This results in 'bold forecasts and timid decisions', where the MOS is underestimated or ignored.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Margin-of-safety conflicts with optionality when strategic branching expands downside exposure without preserving minimum safety buffers.
structured_tensions.source_quote: margin-of-safety vs optionality: margin-of-safety conflicts with optionality when strategic branching expands downside exposure without preserving minimum safety buffers.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Margin-of-safety conflicts with feedback-loops when delayed signals create false confidence in thin buffers.
structured_tensions.source_quote: margin-of-safety vs feedback-loops: margin-of-safety conflicts with feedback-loops when delayed signals create false confidence in thin buffers.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Margin-of-safety conflicts with confirmation-bias when safety estimates are chosen to validate the current plan rather than stress-test it honestly.
structured_tensions.source_quote: margin-of-safety vs confirmation-bias: margin-of-safety conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when safety margin estimates are selected to confirm that the current exposure is acceptable rather than honestly stress-tested against adverse scenarios.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on explicit tensions and the optimism antagonism; both are now wired. Availability-style conflicts remain deferred.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation sprawl where the raw relation surface is thinner than the explicit tension block.
open_questions: Availability-style conflict is source-real but availability-heuristic as a distinct canonical target was not added to avoid overlap with representativeness-heuristic semantics.
